prompt: Return the 12 months of 2023 with total revenue and total units sold; columns: month_start, total_revenue, total_units; order by month_start ASC.
Retriever_1_message:
I executed this query to retrieve the required data:

'''SQL
SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', s.Sold_Date) AS month_start, 
       SUM(s.Total_Sale_Value) AS total_revenue, 
       SUM(s.Qty_Sold) AS total_units 
FROM sales s 
WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM s.Sold_Date) = 2023 
GROUP BY month_start 
ORDER BY month_start ASC;
'''

The resulting DataFrame has 12 rows with columns : month_start, total_revenue, total_units

prompt: Return the top 20 SKUs by total units sold across the full dataset; tie-break by higher total revenue, then SKU_Coded ASC; columns: SKU_Coded, total_units, total_revenue.
Retriever_1_message:
I executed this query to retrieve the required data:

'''SQL
SELECT s.SKU_Coded, SUM(s.Qty_Sold) AS total_units, SUM(s.Total_Sale_Value) AS total_revenue 
FROM sales s 
GROUP BY s.SKU_Coded 
ORDER BY total_units DESC, total_revenue DESC, s.SKU_Coded ASC

prompt: For 2023, return the top 25 stores by promotional unit share (promo_units/total_units) among stores with at least 100 total units; tie-break by Store_Number ASC; columns: Store_Number, promo_unit_share, promo_units, total_units.
Retriever_1_message:
I executed this query to retrieve the required data:

'''SQL
SELECT s.Store_Number, 
       ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN sa.On_Promo = 1 THEN sa.Qty_Sold ELSE 0 END) * 1.0 / NULLIF(SUM(sa.Qty_Sold), 0), 4) AS promo_unit_share, 
       SUM(CASE WHEN sa.On_Promo = 1 THEN sa.Qty_Sold ELSE 0 END) AS promo_units, 
       SUM(sa.Qty_Sold) AS total_units 
FROM sales sa 
JOIN stores s ON sa.Store_Number = s.Store_Number 
WHERE YEAR(sa.Sold_Date) = 2023 
GROUP BY s.Store_Number 
HAVING SUM(sa.Qty_Sold) >= 100 
ORDER BY promo_unit_share DESC, s.Store_Number ASC 
LIMIT 25;
'''

The resulting DataFrame has 25 rows with columns : Store_Number, promo_unit_share, promo_units, total_units
Analyzer_1_message: The top 25 stores by promotional unit share in 2023, among those with at least 100 total units, are led by Store 2860 with a promotional unit share of 0.03, selling 250 promotional units out of 7319 total units. Other notable stores include Store 3190 and Store 770, both also with a promotional unit share of 0.03, selling 360 and 312 promotional units respectively.

prompt: For Q1 2023 (2023-01-01 to 2023-03-31), return the top 30 stores by total revenue; tie-break by Store_Number ASC; columns: Store_Number, total_revenue, total_units.

'''SQL

prompt: For 2023, return the top 10 SKUs by total revenue with avg unit price = sum(Total_Sale_Value)/sum(Qty_Sold); tie-break by SKU_Coded ASC; columns: SKU_Coded, total_revenue, total_units, avg_unit_price.
Retriever_1_message:
I executed this query to retrieve the required data:

'''SQL
SELECT s.SKU_Coded, 
       SUM(s.Total_Sale_Value) AS total_revenue, 
       SUM(s.Qty_Sold) AS total_units, 
       SUM(s.Total_Sale_Value) / NULLIF(SUM(s.Qty_Sold), 0) AS avg_unit_price 
FROM sales s 
WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM s.Sold_Date) = 2023 
GROUP BY s.SKU_Coded 
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC, s.SKU_Coded ASC 
LIMIT 10;
'''

The resulting DataFrame has 10 rows with columns : SKU_Coded, total_revenue, total_units, avg_unit_price

Other notable SKUs include 6184900 with $147,797.62 in revenue and an average unit price of $89.52, and 6194250 with $116,109.58 in revenue and an average unit price of $19.94.

prompt: Across the full dataset, return the 40 SKUs with the highest average unit price among SKUs with at least 50 units sold; avg_unit_price = sum(Total_Sale_Value)/sum(Qty_Sold); tie-break by SKU_Coded ASC; columns: SKU_Coded, avg_unit_price, total_units, total_revenue.
Retriever_1_message:
I executed this query to retrieve the required data:

'''SQL
SELECT s.SKU_Coded, 
       ROUND(SUM(s.Total_Sale_Value) / NULLIF(SUM(s.Qty_Sold), 0), 2) AS avg_unit_price, 
       SUM(s.Qty_Sold) AS total_units, 
       SUM(s.Total_Sale_Value) AS total_revenue 
FROM sales s 
GROUP BY s.SKU_Coded 
HAVING SUM(s.Qty_Sold) >= 50 
ORDER BY avg_unit_price DESC, s.SKU_Coded ASC 
LIMIT 40;
'''

The resulting DataFrame has 40 rows with columns : SKU_Coded, avg_unit_price, total_units, total_revenue
Analyzer_1_message: The 40 SKUs with the highest average unit price among those with at least 50 units sold are led by SKU 6204850, which has an average unit price of $148.91, total units sold of 415, and total revenue of $61,797.71. Other notable SKUs include 6204800 with an average unit price of $113.20 and 6185100 at $99.55. The complete list includes various SKUs with average prices ranging from $148.91 to $43.59.
